_comment_identity: Identifies the target repo. repo_name is the local directory name.
_comment_description: Short description used in system prompts after 'The codebase is ...'
repo_description: a 5G RAN solution with CU/DU implementation
_comment_system_prompt_context: Longer paragraph injected into agent system prompts describing what the repo contains, its language, build system, etc. Written as plain text (no markup).
system_prompt_context: The repository implements a complete 5G radio access network (RAN) solution with CU/DU functionality, primarily in C++. It uses CMake as the build system and includes modular components for L1/2/3 layers, F1/E1/E2AP, and gNB applications.
_comment_scan_dirs: Top-level directories to scan for function extraction.
_comment_subsystem_description: Human-readable subsystem mapping used in training system prompts.
subsystem_description: gNB applications (apps), core libraries (lib), headers (include), tests (tests)
_comment_docker: Docker image prefix and container-internal path where the repo is mounted.
_comment_build_note: Warning shown to agents about build limitations. Set to empty string if full builds work.
build_caveat: Split-8 configuration may require additional hardware-specific dependencies not available in minimal containers.
_comment_functions_file: Path to the extracted functions JSONL, relative to project root.
_comment_bug_prompts_file: Path to the bug prompts JSON, relative to project root. Generated by populate_repo_config.py.
_comment_fallback_demo_pr: Placeholder PR used when no demo_prs/ directory is found. Should be a realistic example from this repo.
fallback_demo_pr:
## Title: Fix buffer overflow in PDCP SDU processing

### Summary
This PR fixes a buffer overflow vulnerability in the PDCP layer when processing SDUs with malformed header extensions. The issue occurs in `lib/src/pdcp/pdcp_entity.cc` where the code incorrectly calculates the header length when processing extension headers, leading to an out-of-bounds read when accessing the SDU data buffer. This can cause crashes or undefined behavior when processing malicious or corrupted PDCP packets.

The vulnerability is triggered when a PDCP PDU contains extension headers with invalid length fields. The parsing logic fails to properly validate that the calculated header offset does not exceed the actual PDU buffer size before accessing the data. This affects both uplink and downlink PDCP processing paths.

The fix adds proper bounds checking before accessing the SDU buffer and ensures that header length calculations account for the total PDU size. Additionally, error handling is improved to gracefully handle malformed packets rather than crashing.

### Changes
- `lib/src/pdcp/pdcp_entity.cc`: Added bounds checking for header extension parsing and validate PDU length before buffer access
- `include/srsran/pdcp/pdcp_entity.h`: Added helper function for safe header length calculation

### Testing
- Added unit tests in `tests/pdcp/test_pdcp_entity.cc` with malformed PDU test cases
- Verified fix with fuzz testing using corrupted PDCP packets
- Ran existing PDCP test suite to ensure no regressions
- Tested with srsUE and srsGNB integration to confirm normal operation
